assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Response provides recurring weekend maintenance window with FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA (Saturday)

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides specific quarterly freeze exclusion commands with explicit 30-day periods and mentions chaining for longer freezes

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Provides specific November audit exclusion command with --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time "2024-11-01T00:00:00Z" and end time

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Explicitly states "No upgrades exclusions are limited to 30 days maximum" and provides chaining example for longer freezes

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly explain that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows; only shows manual upgrade commands without this clarification

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends Extended channel with maintenance windows and explains the combination provides maximum control

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: All exclusion commands include --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start-time, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end-time flags

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Response includes warning about security vs. control trade-off and notes exclusion limits that could lead to falling behind on versions
